requirement.original: 我上次反馈的问题是hardcode为v3不应该，然后agent就过度反应给我直接删除了…… 好的manifest应该保留 继续使用B，但是告诉我为什么会出现这么多大大小小的Bug！必须保证任意情况都可以成功commit！
requirement.clarified: Restore the v3 manifest mechanism that fe9c0f2 over-deleted, AND fix the originating grievance (hardcoded literal 'v3' in the version validator + audit emission). The restored mechanism is an OPTIONAL precision layer integrated into closed-task and force modes; all four always-on safety hooks and multi-session invariants are preserved; doc and impl land in the same change set.
requirement.what: Restore manifest-driven commit path as OPTIONAL precision layer + replace string-alias version validator with named-integer schema (schema_name + schema_version + schema_minor) + factor shared private-index commit core + co-update commit.md docs.
requirement.why: User explicitly wants the manifest precision layer back (over-deletion regret) AND wants the original hardcode-v3 issue actually fixed (not deleted around). Larger goal: industrial-grade agentic commit where every path can succeed.

included: Restore --manifest <json> argv branch in closed-task and force modes
Replace string-alias version validator with schema_name + integer schema_version
Factor run_private_index_commit shared core from run_semantic_plan_commit
Manifest reader hardening (path-hygiene, normalization, binary rejection, base_commit binding, repo_root rejection, task_id binding)
allowed_files derived from POST-APPLY diff with subset assertion against manifest.files
is_other_session over POST-APPLY diff with force-without-task-id skip
Operator-overrideable conventions (CLAUDE_TMPDIR, CLAUDE_LOG_DIR, CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN; commit.sh:80 reads CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN env into the internal PYTHON_BIN shell variable)
commit.md doc co-update with 7 doc surfaces (DOC-1..DOC-6 body sections + DOC-7 YAML frontmatter restoration per BA-QA iter1 / OBJ-1)
excluded: Full binary file commit support (rejection only)
--force real implementation refactor beyond manifest acceptance
End-to-end test harness (optional smoke set only)
incompatible_after future-major negotiation field (deferred per codex)
Edits to the 4 always-on safety hook scripts (zero diff required)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: User's 'why are there so many bugs' is meta-process feedback about the over-reaction → counter-reaction pattern (fe9c0f2 reasoned at L1 about an L3 problem). Recording for user visibility; not a code fix this cycle. The systemic guardrails already exist in agents/ba.md (Reference Integrity tier rules, Forbidden Patterns, layer differentiation guide) — the failure was a procedural skip, not a missing rule.
L4 set-equality enforcement is correct as-is for bridge-mode; closed-task and force modes don't spawn a git commit subprocess so the guard never fires on them. Per architect LC-1, no defect to fix.

Architect arch-2 recommended four-field schema_name + schema_version + schema_minor + incompatible_after protocol. Codex flagged incompatible_after as over-engineered for foreseeable horizon. Spec adopts simplified current-major-only form (W9 defers incompatible_after); documented deviation from architect recommendation accepted on codex+user-need-precision grounds.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: User reports two compounding issues: (1) original 'hardcode v3 不应该' grievance was never actually fixed; (2) agent over-reacted by deleting the entire manifest mechanism in fe9c0f2 — 715+/528- lines rewritten — destroying the precision layer the user depended on.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: fe9c0f2 reasoned at L1 (cosmetic — 'remove the v3 string') about an L3 problem (data — 'replace the string with a named-integer schema constant'). The reasoning skipped the layer-differentiation step required by agents/ba.md Contract D. Layer mismatch in turn meant the fix's scope was 'delete the file the string lives in' rather than 'replace the string with the correct value'. The user's verbatim observation 'agent就过度反应' is exactly the layer-mismatch + scope-expansion failure mode the Contract D novelty-check was designed to prevent.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: fe9c0f2 - semantic commit engine: remove v3 manifest seam
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Agent interpreted 'hardcode v3 不应该' as 'the v3 surface must not exist' (deletion-shaped). Correct interpretation: 'the literal string "v3" must not be the version-acceptance contract; replace it with a named-integer schema'.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Deleting the manifest mechanism removed the precision-layer API the user uses to commit irregular-shaped diffs. The current dev-report-driven path is correct for closed-task semantic commits but does not cover the cases where the user wants to declare exactly which hunks to commit (manifest precision). Loss of this layer means 'manifest required → manifest gone → manual workaround / force-mode pollution' for the affected workflows.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: fe9c0f2 landed 2026-05-10 ('Sun May 10 08:49:08 2026 +0000'); user reported the over-reaction shortly after; this cycle (task-id 20260510-191533) is the restoration response.

Current commit.sh has zero occurrences of manifest|--manifest|schema_version|v3 (observed: grep -c on /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/hooks/commit.sh)
Current commit.md has zero occurrences of same (observed: grep -c)
Backup commit.sh.bak.1778357948 line 351 reads: if str(version).lower() not in {'3', 'v3', 'commit-intent-v3'} (observed: Read tool, lines 340-360)
Backup line 612, 644 emit literal commit_version: 'v3' in audit and log JSON (observed: Read tool, lines 600-645)
Backup line 460 uses /tmp hardcode; current commit.sh:683 uses ${CLAUDE_TMPDIR%/} (observed via parallel reads)

pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:447-460 enforces set-equality between staged and grant.allowed_files (observed: Read tool); manifest restoration must derive allowed_files from applied diff to satisfy this contract (inferred from contract reading)

component_chain.role: argv parser — pre-scan -m, dispatch mode (closed-task / --auto-bulk-bridge / --force); MUST be extended to accept --manifest <path> and read CLAUDE_COMMIT_MANIFEST_DISABLED env

run_semantic_plan_commit — current dev-report-driven commit body; the 10 M5 invariants (read-tree, apply --3way, ls-files-u, fingerprint CAS, preflight + post-CAS sync_real_index_to_commit_paths, staged-list-equality, branch-advance CAS) live here; MUST be factored into a shared run_private_index_commit core
write_v3_patch_bundle (manifest reader) — produces patch_file + meta_file from manifest JSON; the source of restored manifest-side acquisition logic with literals replaced

run_v3_manifest_commit — backup's manifest commit body; NOT the structural source for restoration (current run_semantic_plan_commit is the structural source); used only to identify which manifest-specific steps to add to the shared core (binary rejection, manifest task_id binding, manifest sha audit emission)

L4 grant validator — enforces set-equality between staged and grant.allowed_files; CONSUMER only, not modified this cycle. Closed-task and force modes don't trigger this (no git commit subprocess); bridge mode is the only emitter today.

Slash-command doc / authority surface; currently has zero manifest mentions and zero YAML frontmatter at baseline; MUST add 7 doc surfaces (DOC-1..DOC-6 body sections + DOC-7 YAML frontmatter restoration per BA-QA iter1 / OBJ-1) in the same change set as the commit.sh restoration.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if mode == "force": print("commit.sh: --force cannot infer task ownership safely; use a closed task-id semantic commit", file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(2)
pre_existing_guards.purpose: guards build_semantic_plan_bundle against being entered in force mode (force uses caller-provided -m, not artifact-derived plan); restoration must NOT remove this guard — the manifest path is the way force-mode commits gain precision plan input.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if [ "$mode" = "closed-task" ] && [ -n "$plan_task_for_check" ] && [ "$plan_task_for_check" != "$task_id" ]; then echo "commit.sh: semantic plan task_id ... does not match" >&2; return 2; fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: task_id binding for the dev-report-driven plan; the manifest path MUST add an analogous binding for manifest.task_id (M8 task_id binding rule) preserving this contract.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if [ "$(real_index_fingerprint "$repo_root")" != "$real_index_before" ]; then echo "...shared index changed..." >&2; return 2; fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: real_index_fingerprint CAS gates BEFORE and AFTER branch-advance; M5 invariants 4 + step-before-step-9; the shared core MUST preserve both placements.
cross-task docs/dev artifact filter; the manifest path MUST hoist this into the shared core and run it over the POST-APPLY diff (M7); skip-when-no-task-identity is the codex finding #6 exception.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if re.search(r"(?m)^GIT binary patch$", patch_text): print("commit.sh: binary patches are not supported by the semantic plan path", file=sys.stderr); sys.exit(2)
pre_existing_guards.purpose: anchored binary rejection already exists for the dev-report path; the shared core MUST apply this to the manifest path too (M8 binary rejection).
L3 always-on layer; restoration does NOT alter commit subject so this is unaffected; zero edits allowed.
L2 always-on layer; manifest content does not enter git commit argv so this is unaffected; zero edits allowed.

development_approach.strategy: Two-step refactor in a single commit: (1) factor run_private_index_commit shared core from current run_semantic_plan_commit (NO behavior change; AC3 canonicalized-byte-identical audit shape for dev-report path is the verification); (2) wire manifest source-acquisition helper into shared core with named-integer schema validator, manifest reader hardening, and feature gate. commit.md co-updated in the same change set with 7 doc surfaces: DOC-1..DOC-6 body sections + DOC-7 YAML frontmatter restoration (BA-QA iter1 / OBJ-1).
development_approach.validation_approach: QA runs AC1..AC9 against the cycle-parent baseline $BASELINE_SHA=109c700637824dd11a0f160c84ef042f7fc49005. For AC3 canonicalized-byte-identical proof, materialize complete hooks tree via `git archive` for both baseline and post-refactor (so lib/close-verdict.py is co-located with commit.sh per BASH_SOURCE resolution at commit.sh:84), run both wrappers against the DEV-created fixture at /tmp/claude-test-fixtures/test-T1-${BASELINE_SHA:0:8}/, redact volatile fields per AC3's named list (actual audit keys: timestamp/sid/commit/parent/backup_ref/plan_sha256/plan_id/plan_base_commit/real_index_fingerprint_before|after), capture each per-run audit JSON to a uniquely-named file ($CLAUDE_LOG_DIR/audit-baseline-<run-id>.json and $CLAUDE_LOG_DIR/audit-postref-<run-id>.json) so runs do not overwrite each other, and assert `diff -u` returns empty. For AC8 path-coverage, run the 4 success paths + D.1 parser-level proof; each path starts from a fresh fixture reset (rm -rf the fixture tree, re-init repo to <initial-sha>, redo dirty diff, AND re-copy the DEV-created docs/dev/ fixture artifacts — dev-report-test-T1-*, close-report-test-T1-*, commit-manifest-test-T1-*.json — from a template directory back into <fixture>/docs/dev/; this matches the AC8 per-path-reset clause in the ticket exactly, which is itself referenced by the AC3 Recipe-hygiene block). For AC4 subset assertion, run both the success-subset and failure-superset cases. For AC9 doc-impl alignment, head -4 commit.md to verify DOC-7 frontmatter restoration + grep for DOC-1..DOC-6 body anchors + assert single change-set co-modification via git log. Env vars: CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN (not PYTHON_BIN; commit.sh:80), CLAUDE_TMPDIR, CLAUDE_LOG_DIR, CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, CLAUDE_HOME (for venv/python fallback).

The generic 'description only' default does NOT apply to /commit; DOC-7 in M13 is the authoritative contract.
